analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet announces a live discussion by Members of the European Parliament about concerns on managing citizens' rights after Brexit. It is informational and issue-focused, without evaluative claims about the EU’s unity, success, usefulness, leadership, or global role. Evaluation per narrative: EU1 (EU successful story) – Not promoted; no reference to success, overcoming obstacles, or historical achievements. EU2 (EU united) – Not promoted; it mentions concerns and a discussion, not unity or agreement. EU3 (EU useful for its citizens) – Not promoted; while the topic is citizens' rights, the tweet does not claim the EU is delivering solutions or improving lives; it only notes concerns and discussion. EU4 (EU avant-garde) – Not promoted; no pioneering regulation or leadership is claimed. EU5 (EU global champion of fair causes) – Not promoted; no human rights leadership framing. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world) – Not promoted; no global contribution is asserted. Conclusion: No listed narratives are explicitly promoted.
